依赖条件

  1. 所有房间都必须先升级至基础

  2. 所有房间都必须先升级至卓越

  3. 所有房间都必须先升级至非凡（此脚注指的是“二楼的非凡房间”）

  4. 熏制房非凡升级即为巫师塔/三层研究塔——这会在熏制房非凡花费之外额外花费500（两者必须同时支付，并投入所需的休整模式时间）

  5. 树屋要求庭院维护已扩建后才能建造——它属于一个“自然房间”，并且必须在扩建庭院完成的同时购买，并累计所需休整模式时间

继承收益

* 厨房以及至少足够让英雄睡觉的已清理/修复房间（队伍x3个空间）必须先完成清理/修复，否则他们就需要进行 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.49y9Ec4bDii8pcD3]{Subsist} 才能在行动基地休息，但在完成该 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.49y9Ec4bDii8pcD3]{Subsist} 检定时获得+1环境加值。

** 升级门廊会阻止危险生物（例如鳄鱼）进入地产，你将不再需要安排守夜才能在这里睡觉。

*** 屋顶的初次修复（第1次升级）会使受训的室内任务获得+1环境加值，专家的室内任务获得+2。

可选升级

共有二十三项可分别升级的投资项目。每一项都对应一组供GM使用的按钮。这二十三个升级部分大多与场景中GM可见的按钮以及下表相对应，但有以下例外：

  1. 树屋在费用/休整模式中单独列出，但它是通过庭院系列中的最后一个按钮（非凡）激活的

  2. 码头按钮系列被单独拆分出来以保持脚本更简洁，但它被包含在门廊与框架升级中。只需让这些按钮系列保持一致即可。

  3. 如果GM想在未来为其加入动画图块，则下方列出了传送门，但没有单独对应的按钮。

（1）关于庭院的说明： 玩家对整张地图的可见范围会受到与庭院升级相对应的限制。随着他们对其进行投资，墙壁会发生变化，为他们揭示更多土地。作为GM，你始终会自然地看到全部内容。

（2）关于门廊的说明： “未清理”升级不会带来视觉变化，但它是一个已投资步骤，仅表示楼梯和松动的木板已经修好。GM控制下的“码头”触发图块应与门廊/框架升级配合使用。之所以将这些触发器分开，是为了简化若干用户交互元素。

平面图

行动基地的平面图可以升级为两层。这通过将底层完成至最终升级来实现。

1层

2层

使用地图

这张地图使用了Monk's Active Tiles by IronMonk，并配有超过3000行脚本来自动化升级。这些升级不仅会改变图块，还会改变墙壁、门、灯光、动画、楼梯，并启用其他活动图块。如果你只想更改房间图块图像，我也加入了Multiface Tiles module by Freeze。使用它会向玩家显示每个房间不同的图块图像，但不会改变墙壁、灯光、门等。

这些控制项只有GM能够看到（前提是场景已启用视野）。详情如下。

空白模板

这些只是作为备用放在这里，以便你作为GM想制作更多时使用；它们也展示了脚本底层的分类体系与结构。点击这些按钮不会发生任何变化。

重置

左键点击此图块时，要么会将整个基地重置为“未清理”并将升级按钮设为白色，要么只会将按钮变为白色。

列切换器 - *新增*

这些新切换器允许GM将整张地图切换为所选的升级状态。（它们不会将先前所选切换器对应的升级图块改回白色；如果你需要那种结果，请先点击“重置”，再点击列切换器。）

行标题

按钮系列的行标题会告诉你，其后按钮将调整地图的哪个部分。在当前初始版本中，点击它们不会有任何效果。

升级按钮

当某个按钮被左键点击时，它会自动升级所有与该等级对应的图块、灯光、墙壁、门以及任何其他活动图块。随后该按钮会切换为绿色，以便你知道自己点击过哪些按钮。（重置按钮会把它变回白色。）

注意：如果英雄要将某个房间连续升级多个等级，请按顺序逐个点击，以获得最佳效果。

我使用PF2E图标来表示升级等级：未清理（被动动作图标）、清理（自由动作图标）、基础（单动作图标）、高级（双动作图标）、非凡（三动作图标）。

当你准备升级行动基地的相应部分时，只需左键点击对应按钮。

共用门 | 同步升级

为防止出现重大冲突，建筑规则要求玩家以同步方式升级房间。这意味着玩家不能让某个房间比其他任一房间领先两个升级步骤。自然地，玩家可以让一个房间比其他房间领先一步，但绝不能领先两步。这既能防止整体使用问题，从建筑升级的角度来看也合乎逻辑。有些房间共用门。这确实会带来一些视觉上的挑战（例如，厨房<>餐厅、东侧走廊<>门廊、起居室<>入口），尤其是当相邻房间之一已经升级，而另一个尚未升级时。应该不会出现任何机制上的墙壁/门冲突（例如指示物移动限制），但玩家可能会在一侧看到“门”的图像，尽管它尚未处于“可正常使用状态”。玩家要么可以直接穿过它（例如厨房<>餐厅，当其中一个为基础而另一个不是时），要么他们需要同步升级，才能让新门完全发挥作用（例如入口<>起居室，当其中一个为基础而另一个不是时）。如果你作为GM或你的玩家在遵循这些说明后仍然发现任何问题，请向我报告，我会修复这些脚本。

修正错误

如果你出错了，可以通过两种方式修正。

  1. 使用重置按钮（反应动作图标）——这会重置整张地图

  2. 点击“未清理”按钮（被动动作图标）以重置该图块，然后根据需要依次激活各个按钮。

This section includes the table for costs and skill checks as well as instructions for the GM on how to use the Upgradeable Map tools.

Note: While Vision is enabled, only the GM will be able to see the controls and interact with them.

Upgrading the Property

In order for heroes to upgrade a room, they will need to the VP, the required downtime, the proficiency level, and pass a @Check[type:crafting] with listed DC. (See Table 1.0.) Some upgrades have dependencies on other upgrades while others provide additional benefits to the party. Table 1.0 lists these additional conditions. Your party may also choose to employ a hireling if they wish to expedite upgrades during downtime or improve their success on skill checks. As they complete upgrades and earn Leadership Points, hirelings can complete tasks while their party is adventuring as well (using the same amount of downtime cost)

Lore Skills

While Crafting is the general and broad skill for the listed DC, you can as the GM adjust the DC down with Applicable Unspecific Lore (-2) and Applicable Specific Lore (-5). Examples:

  • Unspecific Lore: Terrain, Region or a Trade (e.g. Engineering)

  • Specific Lore: Beachfront properties, fortifications, or as determined by the GM

Degrees of Success

  1. Critical Success - complete the task in half the time.

  2. Success - expend resources and time and complete the task!

  3. Failure - expend resources and time, but do not complete the task, they may try again.

  4. Critical Failure - expend resources and time, but do not complete the task, they may try again, but the time doubles until next downtime (as they created a mess, busted something, or went about the construction in the wrong way/order).

Dependencies

  1. All Rooms must be upgraded to Basic first

  2. All Rooms must be upgraded to Superb first

  3. All Rooms must be upgraded to Extraordinary (this footnote is referencing the "extraordinary rooms on 2nd floor"

  4. The Smokehouse Extraordinary upgrade is a Wizard Tower/3 Story Research Tower - this costs 500 beyond the smokehouse Extraordinary Costs (both have to be paid at same time and have required downtime)

  5. The Tree House requires the Yard Care to be Expanded to be built - it is a "Nature room" and has to be purchased at same time the Expanded Yard is Completed and have the cumulative downtime

Inherit Benefits

* The Kitchen and at least enough clean/repaired rooms to sleep the heroes (party x3 spaces) must be cleaned/repaired otherwise they will need to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.49y9Ec4bDii8pcD3]{Subsist} to Rest at the base of operations, but get a +1 circumstance bonus when completing the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.49y9Ec4bDii8pcD3]{Subsist} Check.

** Upgrading the porch will deter dangerous creatures (e.g. Crocodiles) from entering the property, you will no longer need to set watch for sleeping here.

*** Initial repair of the roof (1st upgrade) adds a +1 circumstance bonus to Trained, +2 to Expert indoor tasks.

Possible Upgrades

There are twenty three separate investments that can be upgraded. Each of these correspond to a button series for the GM to use. The twenty three sections to upgrade mostly align between the buttons for the GM on the scene and the table below with the following exceptions:

  1. The Tree House is listed separate for costs/downtime, but it is activate by the last button in the Yard series (extraordinary)

  2. The Docks button series was separated to keep the script more simple, but is included in the Porch & Frame upgrades. Just keep these button series aligned.

  3. Portals is listed below if the GM wants to include a future animated tile for them, but there is no separate button for it.

(1) Note on Yard: The players will have a limited view of the entire map corresponding to the Yard upgrades. The walls will change revealing more land for them as they invest in it As the GM, you will always see it naturally.

(2) Note on Porch: The Unclean upgrade will not make a visual change, but is an invested step, which simply means the stairs and loose boards have been repaired. The "Docks" trigger tiles under GM controls should be used in tandem with Porch/Frame upgrades. The triggers are separated to simplify a few elements for user interaction.

Floor Plan

The floor plan of the Base of Operations can be upgraded to have two levels. This is accomplished by completing the lower level to final upgrades.

Floor 1

Floor 2

Using the Map

This map uses Monk's Active Tiles by IronMonk with over 3k lines of script to automate the upgrades, which not only change the tiles, but change the walls, doors, lights, animations, stairs, and enable other active tiles. If you would like to just change a room tile image, I have also included Multiface Tiles module by Freeze. Using this will show the players a different tile image in each room, but it will not change the walls, lights, doors, etc.

The controls are only able to be seen by the GM (so long as Vision is enabled on the scene). Details are below.

Blank Templates

These are just here in case as a GM, you want to make more and they also show the underlining taxonomy and structure of the scripts. These button will not change anything when clicked.

Reset

This tile when left-clicked will either reset the entire base to "Unclean" and set the upgrade buttons to White, or it will just change them to White.

Column Switches - *New*

These new switches allow the GM to change the entire maps to the upgrade selected. (They will not change the upgrade tiles to white for previously selected switches, if you need that result, then click "reset," then the column switch.

Row Headers

The button-series row headers tell you the section of the map the buttons following them will adjust. Clicking them does nothing in this initial release.

Upgrade Buttons

When a button is left-clicked it will automatically upgrade all tiles, lights, wall, doors and any other active tiles that correspond to it level. It will then switch the button to Green so that you know which ones you have clicked. (The reset button will turn it back to white.)

Note: If the heroes are upgrading a room multiple levels, click each one in sequence for best quality.

I am using the PF2E icons to designate the upgrade level: Unclean (Passive Action Icon), Clean (Free Action Icon), Basic (Single Action Icon), Superior (Two Action Icon), Extraordinary (Three Action Icon).

Simply left-click the appropriate button when you are ready to upgrade that part of the base of operations.

Shared Doors | Synchronizing Upgrades

To prevent major conflicts, the building code requires players to upgrade rooms in synchronized fashion. This means players cannot upgrade one room two steps ahead of any other room. Naturally, players may progress a room one step ahead of others, but never two steps. This will prevent overall user issues, but it also is logical from a building upgrade standpoint. Some of the rooms have shared doors. This does introduce some visual challenges (e.g., Kitchen<>Dining, East Hall<>Porch, Living<>Entry) when one adjacent room is upgraded and the other has not yet. There should not be any mechanical wall/door conflicts (e.g., token movement restrictions), but players may see a "door" image on one side even though it is not in "working condition." The player can either pass through it (e.g., Kitchen<>Dining when one is Basic and the other is not) or they need to synchronize the updates for the new door to fully function (Enter<>Living when one is Basic and the other is not). If you as the GM or your players find any issues despite following these instructions, please report them to me and I will fix the scripts.

Fixing Mistakes

If you make an error, you can fix it in two ways.

  1. Either use the Reset Button (Reaction Action Icon) - this will reset entire map

  2. Click the "Unclean" Button (Passive Action Icon) to reset that tile, and then proceed with activating each button in turn as needed.

GM Guide.name: GM 指南 GM Guide
GM Guide.text:
行动基地 - 概要

行动基地由塔米莉作为给英雄们的一种“感谢”奖励，以答谢他们持续提供的帮助。在我的游戏中，我会给他们这件物品：@UUID[Item.qgMxZLqxBgQX9Xjf]{Fish Camp Deed}，并把英雄们的名字加到上面。列出的税金我会在某些休整模式时间段“大约按月”扣除，作为一种按等级平衡奖励的方式。最终，随着基地获得足够多的升级，这项支出会消失。

当英雄们收到地契时，向他们的玩家提供：@UUID[JournalEntry.FP316sZnRx4PnPQx]{Read Me - Housing Upgrade (Players)}，并鼓励你的玩家为他们的基地命名。

版本 23.4.2.1 新增了一张地图，其中玩家曾受到惹是生非的地精骚扰。行动基地地图默认玩家已经“处理”了这些惹事者，而由于英雄们的存在，此地与奥塔里之间的道路也保持相对安全。（你也可以自由使用“Beaten Path”地图来安排其他遭遇、升级或叙事。）在我的游戏中，于休整模式期间，当英雄们选择待在他们的行动基地，但也会进城采购补给、雇用工人或赚取收入时，我通常每 7 天扣除 2 天，以计算往返跋涉所耗费的时间；我发现这有助于把握升级节奏。）

休整模式

对行动基地进行升级和投资是一项新的休整模式行动。如果英雄们没有正确的熟练度等级，他们也可以根据任务雇用一名 @UUID[Item.WUlJr2sIJamQBzXv]{Hireling} 或一名 @UUID[Item.OIQtlHdTqgkBu9U9]{Hireling (Skilled)} 来协助他们！这些升级的设计目的是支援队伍一直成长到 20 级。

快速指南

  1. 英雄们通过完成任务、遭遇及其他由你——GM——决定的方式来获得胜利点（VP）。

  2. 英雄们选择他们想要改善的房间或地产区域，并在休整模式中花费所需的时间和胜利点。

  3. 如果他们满足最小熟练度要求，并且技能检定成功，那么他们就花费指定数量的休整模式时间。

  4. 查阅升级地图表，确认指定花费的休整模式时间和成功标准。

  5. 看着地产逐步改善吧；每次成功投入 VP，都将其转换为领导力点数，并为冒险队伍提供增益。

其他说明

  1. 可升级地图默认将 token vision 设为“no”。这意味着你的玩家棋子将不会受到阻挡视觉线的墙壁影响。我在这张地图上使用这一默认设置，是为了让玩家无论位于地图的哪个位置，都能看到并感受到整体体验。如果你想限制 Token Vision，那么只需配置地图，并在 Lighting 标签页下启用 Token Vision。

Base of Operations - Summary

The Base of Operations is provided as a reward from Tamily to the heroes as a "thank you" or sorts for their continued assistance. In my games, I provide them this item: @UUID[Item.qgMxZLqxBgQX9Xjf]{Fish Camp Deed} and add the heroes names to it. The listed taxes I deduct "monthly" around certain downtime intervals as a way to balance rewards by level. Eventually this goes away with enough upgrades to the base.

When the heroes receive the Deed, provide their players: @UUID[JournalEntry.FP316sZnRx4PnPQx]{Read Me - Housing Upgrade (Players)} and encourage your players to name their base.

Version 23.4.2.1 added a new Map in which the players were harassed by trouble-making Goblins. The Base of Operations map assumes the players "dealt" with such trouble-makers, and the path between here and Otari stays relatively safe due to the presence of the heroes. (Feel free to use the "Beaten Path" map for other encounters, upgrades or narratives.) In my games during Downtime, when the heroes choose to stay at their base of operations but also go into town for supplies, workers, or to earn income, I usually remove 2 days for every 7 days to account for the trek back and forth; I have found this to help with the pace of upgrading.)

Downtime

Upgrading and investing in the Base of Operations is a new Downtime Activity. The heroes can also hire a @UUID[Item.WUlJr2sIJamQBzXv]{Hireling} or a @UUID[Item.OIQtlHdTqgkBu9U9]{Hireling (Skilled)} depending on the task to assist them if they do not have the correct proficiency level! The upgrading is intended to support the party progress through Level 20.

Quick Guide

  1. Heroes earn Victory Points (VP) by completing quests, encounters, and other ways as determined by you, the GM.

  2. Heroes select a room or part of the property they wish to improve and spend the required time and victory points during downtime.

  3. If they meet the Minimum Proficiency Requirement and are successful at the Skill Check, then they spend the designated amount of downtime.

  4. Reference the Upgrade Map Table for designated downtime spent and success criteria.

  5. Watch the property improve and with every successful VP investment, convert it to Leadership Points and provide perks to the adventuring party.

Other Notes

  1. The Upgradeable Map has a default token vision set to "no." This means your player tokens will not have walls that block line of sight. I use this default on this map so that players can see and feel the experience from any location on the map. If you'd like to restrict Token Vision, then simply configure the map and enable the Token Vision under the Lighting Tab.

Reputation Integration.name: 声望整合 Reputation Integration
Reputation Integration.text:
概述

本模组将声望作为一种奖励，用于追踪你在推进行动基地时（以及GM可能决定的其他方式）与各个团体互动的行为，不论这些行为对他们是有利还是不利。完整规则请参见 Paizo 的《Gamemastery Guide》第164页。

我通常会采取较为保守的做法，并遵循第164页列出的恩惠与损害指引。

初始声望

本模组默认冒险队伍起始时处于“被忽视”状态，但GM当然可以根据他们的战役进行调整。

声望等级

冒险者未必总是知道自己在某个组织中的声望等级，但一般而言会以 被忽视 开始，不过也可能有所不同。英雄可以进行 @Check[type:Society] 对抗意志DC，以判断某个组织的态度，或者对其进行 @UUID[Item.uBWfoc0SXehQhHDa]{搜集信息}。

当GM通过故事叙述或行动基地的改善提供声望奖励时，他们可以提供这些效果，以便玩家切换开启/关闭相应的环境加值。

@UUID[Item.6oeacKC81NErkrhO]{崇敬：奥塔里}, @UUID[Item.cBcmS0Kx5U1R8Cq2]{仰慕：奥塔里}, @UUID[Item.GNYXWKuCtjxkLkYN]{喜欢：奥塔里}, @UUID[Item.npwxPAx1FJypO9VL]{厌恶：奥塔里}, @UUID[Item.b4fytD0f8KutkkY8]{憎恨：奥塔里}, @UUID[Item.iqdig8t5XT66zoXV]{追猎：奥塔里}

GM可能需要手动调整买卖货物时的价格。最后，随着英雄在奥塔里的地位提升，GM也可以选择秘密降低某个与其竞争的组织对奥塔里的声望（例如，寇托斯财团），从而引入其他故事钩子。

被忽视

你并未进入该团体的视线，或者他们对你无所谓。

  • 点数范围： -4 到 4

  • 提升方式： 任何恩惠

  • 降低方式： 任何损害

喜欢

许多成员都知道你，其中几位成员对你是 @UUID[Item.ofQSz0CqaxV1fcpo]{友善} 的。

  • 点数范围： 5 到 14

  • 提升方式： 中等或重大恩惠

  • 降低方式： 任何损害

  • 外交环境加值： +1

仰慕

大多数成员都知道你，其中几位对你是 @UUID[Item.flXhHT7MaYLWMKiC]{乐于助人} 的。

  • 点数范围： 15 到 29

  • 提升方式： 重大恩惠

  • 降低方式： 任何损害

  • 外交环境加值： +3

崇敬

每一位成员都知道你，而且每位成员对你都是 @UUID[Item.flXhHT7MaYLWMKiC]{乐于助人} 的。你会受到赞颂。对他们而言你们就是英雄。这个团体愿意为你承担重大风险。

  • 点数范围： 30 到 50

  • 提升方式： 重大恩惠

  • 降低方式： 中等或重大损害

  • 外交环境加值： +5

厌恶

你在这个团体的几位成员中名声很差。许多成员都知道你，而且通常会对你表现得 @UUID[Item.ANj1i07xsFfpkAhM]{不友善}。

  • 点数范围： -5 到 -14

  • 提升方式： 任何恩惠

  • 降低方式： 中等或重大损害

  • 外交环境加值： -1

憎恨

你已经招致了这个团体的愤怒。绝大多数成员都知道你，并对你抱有极其负面的看法。许多成员对你是 @UUID[Item.k55Bhk6WUOvq8f1p]{敌对} 的。

  • 点数范围： -15 到 -29

  • 提升方式： 任何恩惠

  • 降低方式： 重大损害

  • 外交环境加值： -3

追猎

即使要让该团体付出巨大代价，他们也会积极追猎你。每一位成员都听说过你，并且对你抱持 @UUID[Item.k55Bhk6WUOvq8f1p]{敌对} 态度，决意承担重大风险来毁灭你或阻挠你。

  • 点数范围： -30 到 -50

  • 提升方式： 中等或重大恩惠

  • 降低方式： 重大损害

  • 外交环境加值： -5

声望奖励

这些地位会影响广泛的“社交遭遇”，从搜集信息到留下印象、赎罪，或者只是回忆信息、教授PC一个法术、学识或配方。

支援与协助

从NPC处获得支援，或在雇工或受训专家方面获得折扣

外交与社会

在 @Check[type:diplomacy] 或 @Check[type:society] 的技能检定中获得环境加值

专长与学识

新的专长或学识技能可能会对你开放，或者你可能学会新的社交学识技能

恩惠与禁忌

如果你的声望对应的是某个教会或神祇，你可能获得环境加值（或需要通过赎罪来消除的减值）

Overview

Reputation is offered in this module as a reward for progressing the Base of Operations (and as other ways the GM may determine) as a way to track your acts of engagement whether they are favorable or unfavorable to groups. Please see Paizo's Gamemastery Guide Page 164 for full rules.

I usually am conservative and align to the Favor and Disservice guidelines listed on Pgae 164.

Starting Reputation

This module assumed the adventuring party starts as "ignored," but the GM can of course adjust this based on their campaign.

Reputation Levels

Adventurers may not always know the reputation level with an organization, but generally start as ignored but could be different. Heroes may perform @Check[type:Society] against the Will DC to gauge an organizations outlook or @UUID[Item.uBWfoc0SXehQhHDa]{Gather Information} about them.

As the GM offers Reputation as rewards from story narrative or through the improvement of the Base of Operations, they can supply these effects to allow the players to toggle on/off the appropriate circumstance bonuses.

@UUID[Item.6oeacKC81NErkrhO]{Revered: Otari}, @UUID[Item.cBcmS0Kx5U1R8Cq2]{Admired: Otari}, @UUID[Item.GNYXWKuCtjxkLkYN]{Liked: Otari}, @UUID[Item.npwxPAx1FJypO9VL]{Disliked: Otari}, @UUID[Item.b4fytD0f8KutkkY8]{Hated: Otari}, @UUID[Item.iqdig8t5XT66zoXV]{Hunted: Otari}

The GM may need to manually adjust pricing for the buying/selling of goods. Finally, the GM may choose to secretly decrease the reputation of a competitive organization to Otari (e.g, Kortos Consortium) as the heroes increase their Otari Standing, which could introduce other story hooks.

ignored

You are not on the group's radar or they are ambivalent to you.

  • Point Range: -4 to 4

  • Raised by: Any favor

  • Lowered by: Any disservice

Liked

You are known by many members, and several members are @UUID[Item.ofQSz0CqaxV1fcpo]{Friendly} toward you.

  • Point Range: 5 to 14

  • Raised by: Moderate or major favor

  • Lowered by: Any disservice

  • Diplomacy Circumstance Bonus: +1

admired

You are known by most of the members, and several are @UUID[Item.flXhHT7MaYLWMKiC]{Helpful} toward you.

  • Point Range: 15 to 29

  • Raised by: Major Favor

  • Lowered by: Any disservice

  • Diplomacy Circumstance Bonus: +3

revered

You are known by every member, and every member is @UUID[Item.flXhHT7MaYLWMKiC]{Helpful} toward you. You are celebrated. You are heroes to them. The group would take major risks for you.

  • Point Range: 30 to 50

  • Raised by: Major favor

  • Lowered by: Moderate or major disservice

  • Diplomacy Circumstance Bonus: +5

Disliked

You have a poor reputation amongst several members of this group. Many members know you, and are usually @UUID[Item.ANj1i07xsFfpkAhM]{Unfriendly} to you.

  • Point Range: -5 to -14

  • Raised by: Any favor

  • Lowered by: Moderate or Major disservice

  • Diplomacy Circumstance Bonus: -1

hated

You have earned this groups ire. The vast majority know you, and have an extremely unfavorable opinion of you. Many members are @UUID[Item.k55Bhk6WUOvq8f1p]{Hostile} to you.

  • Point Range: -15 to -29

  • Raised by: Any favor

  • Lowered by: Major disservice

  • Diplomacy Circumstance Bonus: -3

hunted

You are actively hunted even at a great cost to the group. Every member has heard of you and is @UUID[Item.k55Bhk6WUOvq8f1p]{Hostile} toward you and is determined to take major risks to destroy or thwart you.

  • Point Range: -30 to -50

  • Raised by: Moderate or major favor

  • Lowered by: Major disservice

  • Diplomacy Circumstance Bonus: -5

Reputation rewards

These standings can impact a wide range of "social encounters" from Gathering Information, to Making Impressions, Atoning or simply recalling information or teaching the PCs a spell, lore, or formula.

Support & Assistance

Earn support from NPCs or discounts on hirelings or trained specialist

Diplomacy & Society

Circumstance bonuses to skill checks in @Check[type:diplomacy] or @Check[type:society]

Feats & Lore

New feats or lore skills may become available to you or you may learn new social lore skills

Favor & Anathema

If your reputation is with a Church or Deity, you may earn circumstance bonus (or penalties requiring atonement)

Encounter Map.name: 遭遇地图 Encounter Map
Encounter Map.text:
海滩营地

这张地图支持《Troubles in Otari》（TiO）独立冒险第一章“Fishing for Adventure”。你需要购买该冒险才能使用这张遭遇地图。

此外，我也建议你购买《Bestiary》的模组。这里面的指示物使用了那个模组，但即使没有它也可以运行。

设置地图

  1. 加载该冒险的 PDF（在你从 Paizo 购买之后）。

  2. 更新所有指示物摆放位置（我已经把它们全部预先放好并隐藏了）。我使用这张地图时，会在玩家打开每一扇门时让他们暂停并进行一次察觉检定。

  3. 自动暂停地块——你的玩家里是否有人有时会比其他人移动得更快？这张地图包含暂停地块，会强制激活暂停按钮，并在进入每个房间前要求进行一次察觉检定。我也附带了地块指示物，这样如果你想添加更多，也可以直接使用。

  4. 发现区域——这里有三个“发现区域”，使用的是 Monk's Active Tile Triggers。你可以在屏幕右侧的菜单中重置其中任意一个。你需要正确设置 Macro 才能让它们生效，不过这一步很简单（见下文）。

    鳄鱼——当 PC 进入指定区域时，系统会要求进行一次察觉检定。如果你的游戏世界中有这个 Macro（见下文），它会根据结果自动显示对应的结果（通常是踪迹）。

    蜈蚣——如果 PC 与渔网互动，它会自动触发战斗并要求掷先攻。如果他们在没有碰触渔网的情况下进入该区域，则会允许他们进行一次察觉检定。Macro 会再次触发并显示正确的结果。

    办公室——当英雄进入这个空间时，macro 会触发并自动处理结果。你也可以把物品链接到这些内容上（我在自己的私人游戏中就是这么做的，但我在这个免费版本中移除了它们）。

  5. 灯光——PF2E 有一些内置照明功能，这会让在房屋内外、处于微光或黑暗时的处理对 GM 来说有些麻烦。我在 GM 控制区域里加入了灯光开关，你可以用它关闭室外的所有灯光。你还需要这样做，并把场景调整为黑暗，才能移除屋内的光源。

  6. 动态地块——我只使用 JB2A。这里面的所有内容应该都包含在免费版本中。我已经把它设为依赖项，但你可能还是想再确认一下。这张地图上的蜘蛛网、软泥、坍塌的门廊和坍塌的楼梯都使用了它们。

  7. 音效——我只使用 MGS，不过因为这是免费内容，我已经移除了 MGS 音效。如果你支持 MGS（而且你确实应该支持），你需要按需自行添加 SFX 和环境音。

导入 Macro

我使用的 Macro 很少，但下面列出的这个非常棒。它会在你导入冒险时自动下载到你的世界中，不过如果你愿意，也可以自行调整 DC。（你也可以在 Baileywiki Nuts & Bolts 中找到它，它的名字叫“Multiple Anchors based on DC.”）

调整 DC 阶段

  1. 找到你想要调整的地块，然后进入 Actions 标签页

  2. 向下滚动动作列表，直到你在脚本序列中找到“Run Macro”动作。

  3. 现在使用你的快捷栏文件夹打开 Macro 文件夹，搜索“DC”或“TiO”

  4. 找到与该地块触发器中所用 Macro 对应的那个 Macro，右键单击并复制它

  5. 在脚本字段中调整所有落点的 DC，然后保存

  6. 我喜欢把这些 Macro 的变体另存为稍有变化的名称，比如“DC15”“DC20”或者带有“(text here)”的名字

  7. 现在返回 MATT，找到之前列出“Run Macro”动作的位置并编辑它

  8. 将更新后的 Macro 链接到你新保存的那个

  9. 保存编辑，然后

  10. 保存地块/发现区域——现在你就准备好了！

Beach Camp

This map supports the Troubles in Otari (TiO) Standalone Adventure Chapter 1 called "Fishing for Adventure." You will need to purchase the adventure to make use of the Encounter Map.

Additionally, I encourage you to pick up the Module for the Bestiary. The tokens within here use that Module, but it can work without it.

Setting Up the Map

  1. Load the PDF for the adventure (after you have purchased it from Paizo).

  2. Update any token placements (I have them all preplaced and hidden). When I use the map, I have players do a pause and perception check when they open each door.

  3. Automatic Pause Tiles - do you have players who sometimes move quickly ahead of others? This map has pause tiles that force the pause button to activate and call for a perception check before entering each room. I have included the Tile-tokens so that if you want to add more, you can.

  4. Discover Zones - there are three "Discovery Zones" that use Monk's Active Tile Triggers. You can reset any of these in the menu on the right side of the screen. You will need to set up the Macro correctly for these to work, it is an easy step (see below).

    Crocodiles - When the PC enter the designated area it will ask for a perception check. If you have the Macro in your game world (see below), it will automatically show the results (usually tracks ) relative to the result

    Centipedes - If the PC interacts with the fishing nets, it will automatically trigger combat and call for initiative. If they enter the area without touching the nets, it will allow them to do a perception check. The Macro will trigger again and show the correct results.

    Office - When the Heroes enter this space, the macro will fire and perform the automation of results. You can link items to these as well (I have them in my personal games, but removed them from this free version).

  5. Lights - PF2E has some integrated lighting that can make being inside/outside of a house with low-light or darkness a challenge for the GM. I have included light-switches in the GM control area where you can switch all lights off outside. You will need to do this and adjust scene to darkness to remove light from within the house.

  6. Animated Tiles - JB2A is the only ones I use. All of the ones within here should be in the free version. I have made it a dependency, but you may want to double check these. The spider webs, ooze, collapsed porch and collapsed stairs use them on this map.

  7. Sounds - I use MGS only, but since this is free, I have removed MGS sounds. If you support MGS (and you should), you will need to add SFX and Ambience as desired.

Importing the Macro

I use very few macros, but the one listed below is amazing. It will automatically download to your world with the adventure import, but you can also adjust the DCs if you prefer. (You can also find it in Baileywiki Nuts & Bolts, and it is called "Multiple Anchors based on DC.")

Adjusting the DC Steps

  1. Locate the tile you want to adjust and go to the Actions Tab

  2. Scroll down the action list until you find the "Run Macro" action in the script sequences.

  3. Now open them Macro folder by using your hot-bar folder by search "DC" or "TiO"

  4. Find the Macro that matches the one in the tile trigger and right click on it duplicate it

  5. Adjust the DC in the script fields for all landings and then save it

  6. I like to save my variations of this macros with a slight change like "DC15" or "DC20" or with a "(text here)" name

  7. Now navigate back to the MATT to where "Run Macro" action was listed and edit this

  8. Link the updated Macro to the new one you saved

  9. Save the Edit, and

  10. Save the Tile/Discovery Zone - now you are ready!
